title: Multiply 3- and 4-Digit Numbers with Regrouping
essential_question: How do you multiply a 3- or 4-digit number by a 1-digit number?

hints: Think about what multiply 3 4 digit regrouping means.
Try drawing an array or using place value.

feedback.incorrect: Not quite. The answer is 936. Multiply place by place.

hints: Think about what multiply 3 4 digit regrouping means.

feedback.incorrect: Not quite. The answer is 6170. Multiply place by place.

hints: Multiplication is repeated addition. Use known facts when possible.

pretest.question: When multiplying 4-digit numbers, you may regroup in more than one column.

feedback.incorrect: The answer is True.

hints: Multiplication is repeated addition. Use known facts when possible.
Think about what multiply 3 4 digit regrouping means.
Try drawing an array or using place value.

feedback.incorrect: Not quite. The answer is 20868. Multiply place by place.

learn.title: Scale Up
learn.content: Multiplying 3- or 4-digit numbers works the same way as 2-digit. Multiply each place, regroup, and add.
learn.tts: Multiplying 3- or 4-digit numbers works the same way as 2-digit. Multiply each place, regroup, and add.

learn.title: Work Right to Left
learn.content: Start from the ones place, move to tens, hundreds, thousands. Regroup whenever a column has 10 or more.
learn.tts: Start from the ones place, move to tens, hundreds, thousands. Regroup whenever a column has 10 or more.

learn.title: Keep Columns Neat
learn.content: Line up the digits carefully so that tens add to tens and hundreds to hundreds. Messy columns cause mistakes.
learn.tts: Line up the digits carefully so that tens add to tens and hundreds to hundreds. Messy columns cause mistakes.

learn.title: Partial Products as a Check
learn.content: You can also use partial products to verify standard algorithm answers.
learn.tts: You can also use partial products to verify standard algorithm answers.

learn.title: Estimate First
learn.content: Always estimate first. It's your reasonableness check.
learn.tts: Always estimate first. It's your reasonableness check.
